title: nepeta sulphurea subsp. lydiae
intro: Nepeta sulphurea subsp. lydiae, also known as Lydia catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a sedative and to ease digestive issues. The health benefits associated with Lydia catmint include its potential to reduce anxiety and promote a good night's sleep, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of Nepeta sulphurea subsp. lydiae often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness the plant's medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
